facade_colour: cream-painted brick and stucco with gray-taupe trim and a darker recessed porch

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: cream-painted brick and stucco with gray-taupe trim and a darker recessed porch
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Older house-form facade is weathered and partially obscured by leafless branches, with a sagging wood front fence and modest deferred maintenance visible at the porch and yard.
photo_observations.window_details: Tall narrow rectangular windows flank the central gable, with one small round-arched attic opening and a canted bay window at the ground floor.
photo_observations.door_details: Single residential door is recessed within a shallow porch and partly hidden by shadow and branches.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple painted trim at the eaves and gable edges.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: One shallow canted bay window projects at the ground floor.
photo_observations.porch_details: Small recessed front porch behind a low, deteriorated wood fence.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible in the front view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Round-arched attic opening, light molded trim, and the front-facing gable give the house a modest Victorian character.
photo_observations.signage_observed: No clear building signage; a small notice is posted in the bay window.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Painted masonry, replacement windows, and altered porch/fence elements modify the original facade.
photo_observations.notes: Best visible from IMG_20260315_153442282_HDR.jpg, though branches obscure part of the elevation.

notes: Best visible from IMG_20260315_153442282_HDR.jpg, though branches obscure part of the elevation.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a front-gabled roof, consistent with its house-form, row, bay-and-gable typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.

facade_description: Brick house, Bellevue area presents a bay-and-gable domestic facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a front-gabled roof, consistent with its house-form, row, bay-and-gable typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a cream-painted brick and stucco with gray-taupe trim and a darker recessed porch palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
